iti101:1.1: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti101:2.1: “Monks, these four things are next to nothing, both easy to gain & blameless.
iti101:2.3: Cast-off cloth is next to nothing, both easy to gain & blameless.
iti101:2.4: Alms food is next to nothing, both easy to gain & blameless.
iti101:2.5: The root of a tree as a dwelling place is next to nothing, both easy to gain & blameless.
iti101:2.6: Medicine made of smelly urine is next to nothing, both easy to gain & blameless.
iti101:2.7: These are the four things that are next to nothing, both easy to gain & blameless.
iti101:2.8:
When a monk is content with what is next to nothing, easy to gain & blameless,
then I say that he has one of the component factors of the contemplative life.”
iti101:3.2: next to nothing, easy to gain,
iti101:3.6: the four directions offer him no obstruction.
